title: dracaena serrulata subsp. mccoyorum
intro: Dracaena serrulata subsp. mccoyorum, also known as McCoy's dracaena, is a plant species in the family Asparagaceae, native to the mountainous regions of Mexico and Central America. The medicinal properties of this plant include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits associated with McCoy's dracaena include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, coughs, and other upper respiratory infections.
